page: Microsoft Azure D96as_v5 is a general-purpose server belonging to the DASv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores on the x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The server is configured with 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include any GPUs or local storage, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core CPU performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core tests and passmark CPU encryption. Single-core performance is strong, with stress-ng single-core and passmark CPU scores in the top 25%. Memory performance is mixed; the passmark memory score ranks in the top 10%, and small-block cached read bandwidth is strong, but large-block uncached read and write bandwidths fall into the average category. The system also demonstrates strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, software compilation, and ray tracing capabilities.

From a resource density perspective, the D96as_v5 offers high compute density with a balanced memory profile, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for parallelized workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. The lack of local storage requires relying on external network-attached storage, which is a key architectural tradeoff. This server is aligned with workloads such as multi-threaded software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and medium-scale language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D96as_v5 is a general-purpose server from the DASv5 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 96 vCPUs, 48 physical cores, and 384.0 GB of RAM. Running on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it features a 3.525 GHz clock speed but lacks local storage and GPU hardware. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance and encryption capabilities, alongside strong single-core and memory scores. However, large-block uncached memory bandwidth remains average. Qualitatively, this server offers high resource density for parallel processing without the added cost of bundled local storage. It is suited for multi-threaded software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96as_v5 is a general-purpose server featuring 96 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7763 processor. It delivers top-tier multi-core performance for database and multi-threaded workloads.

tagline: A ninety-six vCPU AMD EPYC server with balanced memory, optimized for parallelized multi-threaded processing and database workloads.

384.0 GB of RAM providing a balanced 4.0 GB per vCPU ratio
